description:
Paladin Capital Group is a leading global investor that supports and grows the world’s most innovative companies through venture investment, expansion, and growth capital. We are headquartered in Washington, DC, with offices in London, Luxembourg, New York City, and Silicon Valley. We seek out diverse investment opportunities across the US and worldwide.

Paladin is a leader in investing in technologies, products, and services focused on dual use in both commercial and governmental markets with a strong value-add culture.

Paladin Cyber Fund: Our most recent fund focuses on Digital Infrastructure Resilience, investing globally in advanced technologies and solutions which enable, monitor, manage, and defend critical infrastructure that is dependent on cyber space. The Fund is actively investing and has closed eleven investments.

NEW: Europe’s best-funded defence techs eye acquisitions as they race to commercialise

---

Europe’s hottest defence tech startups are raising billions of euros — and some are increasingly using the cash to acquire other companies.

Defence tech unicorn Helsing’s announcement it had bought underwater autonomous vehicles startup Blue Ocean last week was its second announced acquisition of the year. Surveillance drones company Quantum Systems has taken over three companies in 2025. Drone makers Stark and Tekever have also been active.

The M&A splurge comes as Europe’s increasingly well-funded defence techs look to bolster their capabilities and beat rivals to rapidly opening revenue opportunities in the region. 

Defence tech is in the middle of a boom right now. Investors have funneled €3.5bn into the sector so far this year, according to Sifted data, a sharp rise from the €1.85bn picked up in 2024. 

Governments across Europe have pledged to ramp up defence spending in the face of rising geopolitical tensions, with some of them signalling a growing willingness to open up lucrative defence contracts to startups. 

That’s led some of Europe’s best-funded defence startups to ramp up M&A in 2025, expanding into new segments or launching new products, as they look to take advantage of an uptick in the addressable market. 

“There’s an opportunity here to build an alt-prime,” says Nazo Moosa, managing director at defence tech VC Paladin Capital Group, referring to a new version of the incumbent defence primes which have longstanding direct relationships with governments. “To do that you need lots of capabilities.”

Full story with Anne Sraders: https://lnkd.in/epTXbizv

🎉 Today, Paladin strategic advisors Jamil Jaffer and Chris Inglis participated in a panel discussion at @Claroty's Nexus 2025 Conference about cybersecurity risks to the U.S. supply chain alongside Dave DeWalt of NightDragon. 
 
Jamil and Chris took the stage to discuss the likely trajectory of cybersecurity regulation and the national strategy needed to build strong digital infrastructure and supply chains. 
 
The audience featured other industry leaders, cybersecurity experts, and policymakers working to tackle the most pressing challenges of today's cyber landscape. 
 
Thank you to Claroty Chief Strategy Officer Grant Geyer for introducing the segment and to Jamil and Chris for sharing your insights! 
 
#Nexus2025

company_updates.description:
We’re proud to see our portfolio company Semperis continue to lead the charge in identity resilience this time through a documentary.

“Midnight in the War Room” brings together Paladin's own Chris Inglis and Prof. Mary Aiken PhD, alongside top defenders and reformed hackers, to reveal what really happens when the world’s most critical systems are under attack. It’s a powerful reminder that cyber defense isn’t just technology.

Congratulations to the Semperis team for sparking a broader conversation about the stakes of modern cyber conflict.
company_updates.reshared_post_description:
Every day, defenders fight a war in cyberspace.

Midnight in the War Room — a first-of-its-kind documentary — tells their stories.

Featuring over 50 experts from around the world — global CISOs, reformed hackers, military strategists, cyber psychologists, journalists, and victims — the film exposes the human side of cyber war and what’s truly at stake.

For years, we’ve said cybersecurity is everyone’s problem. This film shows why.

Watch the official trailer now.

Coming spring 2026. Visit midnightinthewarroom.com to learn more.

#MidnightInTheWarRoom #DefendersUnite

company_updates.reshared_post_description:
I'm excited to head out to Dubai next week to join World Economic Forum’s Annual Meetings of the Global Future Councils and Cybersecurity. In the lead up to the event, I've published a blog post with Kuan Seah CHUA and Dawn Song on the ways to enhance cybersecurity defenses from attackers who weaponize artificial general intelligence (AGI).  
 
Some key insights:  
⭐️An uneven contest: Attackers enjoy inherent advantages and security operations must defend against every potential weakness in a system.  
⭐️A double-edged sword: Adversaries often move faster than our defensive capabilities in adopting AGI. Defenders must accelerate their use of AI to combat this offense-defense asymmetry. 
⭐️Call to action: Stakeholders should work to secure the AI tech stack and take a life cycle approach to securing AI systems. International and industry cooperation will be crucial in this fight. 
 
Read more: https://lnkd.in/eZBZnDqb

Industry experts emphasized the need to prepare for cyberattacks and data breaches — and warned the threats are coming faster than many expect. 

Guests included Sen. The briefing also featured executive insights from Cy4Data Labs CEO and Co-Founder Lance Smith.

A special thank you to our sponsor – Cy4Data Labs – for their support.

🟣 Most enterprise AI deployments struggle not because of ambition, but because they fail to capture the realities of how work actually happens, leaving even sophisticated AI agents without the context to perform. 

With more than 30 large enterprise customers, including Fortune 500 leaders, Mimica is already setting a new standard for AI impact. By recording and mapping the daily work of employees, Mimica delivers the context that AI agents need to automate complex business processes, unlocking millions in time and cost savings for global enterprises.

📈 This momentum is now matched by a new $26.2M investment led by Paladin Capital Group to accelerate Mimica’s mission and help even more enterprises move from internal pilots to high-impact, production-ready automation.

💡 “As the demands on enterprise AI grow, Mimica is proving that deep process knowledge - and the ability to turn it into actionable automation - is what sets next-generation innovators apart,” said Gibb Witham, Senior Vice President at Paladin Capital Group. “Mimica’s customer track record speaks for itself. We look forward to seeing Mimica enable even more organizations to move from internal pilots to real-world impact.”

Read the announcement here: https://lnkd.in/esvR8mcy

company_updates.description:
In this fascinating conversation with Fund Shack’s Ross Butler, our Strategic Advisor, Alex van Someren, draws on decades at the nexus of deep tech, venture capital and national security. 

📻 Tune in to hear from the former UK Chief Scientific Adviser for National Security as he shares:

➡️ Why partnerships between government, investors, and startups are essential for critical technologies in cybersecurity, AI, and defence
➡️ Candid insights on public and private sector culture - and the importance of risk-taking for innovation
➡️ Where the UK and Europe excel in advanced tech, and why global collaboration matters more than ever

Today more than ever, we need an empowered leader to strengthen our national cybersecurity strategy and enhance private and public sector collaboration to address cyber threats facing the U.S. and our allies.
  
This week, two of Paladin’s Strategic Advisory Group members, RADM (Ret.) Mark Montgomery and former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis, urged members of the U.S. Senate to confirm Sean Cairncross as the nation’s next National Cyber Director.  

Paladin joins our distinguished advisors in supporting this nomination. Cairncross has a strong track record of public service and has demonstrated his commitment to advancing our U.S. national security.   

“There has never been such an important moment to have top leadership in place to drive the defense of America’s critical infrastructure from nation states and criminal gangs who each day initiate cyber-attacks to steal, disrupt, and destroy. Sean Cairncross is the right leader for the job, and we urge his quick confirmation as the next National Cyber Director,” said Michael Steed, Founder and Managing Partner of the Paladin Capital Group. 

Read Steed's statement from February following the announcement of Cairncross' nomination: https://lnkd.in/e_hKP5kj

Today, Paladin portfolio company and leader in Arm device virtualization Corellium announced its agreement to be acquired by Cellebrite, a powerhouse in digital intelligence and investigative analytics. Corellium helps developer and cybersecurity teams build, test, and secure mobile and IoT apps, firmware, and hardware through the power of virtualization.  

Paladin has recognized the unique potential of Corellium to secure customers’ mobile devices, leading a $25 million Series A round in 2021. The acquisition will have an enterprise value of up to $200 million, resulting in excellent returns on capital for Paladin’s investors. 

Corellium Co-Founder and CEO Amanda Gorton shared: “Paladin’s support played a pivotal role in helping us scale our technology and our mission — and ultimately, in reaching this milestone with Cellebrite. We’re deeply grateful for their belief in us.”

European defence budgets are going up. With TechCrunch, Nazo Moosa addresses the question, can startups compete with traditional players in this fast-growing sector?

➡️ Ukraine showed defence departments that they need to change – 10-year procurement cycles are no longer acceptable.

➡️ Just like B2C models adapted to B2B, B2B startups are turning to B2G (government), and governments are turning to SMBs for solutions.

➡️ Even as Europe strives to improve its resilience, the US market will remain key for the foreseeable future.

Watch the video to hear more about Paladin’s assessment of the current climate for entrepreneurs developing dual-use technologies.
 
#investing #cyber #dualuse

Cloud security is a major headache for organisations overwhelmed with alerts that they need to manually triage.

We’ve led a $6M investment in Circumvent, a next-gen cloud security platform based on a multi-agent AI system. Its network of intelligent agents autonomously manages complex prioritisation and remediation tasks, helping cloud and security teams cut through the noise.

Tom Clute, who led the investment for Paladin, said: “Circumvent is addressing one of the most critical challenges in cloud security today: closing the gap between identifying risks and driving effective, scalable remediation.”

We’re delighted to once again be backing Michael Watts, who founded Cloud Conformity, a Paladin portfolio company that Trend Micro acquired in 2019. Michael co-founded Circumvent with Thomas Bui, CTO, who previously held roles across banking, government and at Uber. 

Find out more about Sydney and San Francisco-based Circumvent’s plans here:  
https://lnkd.in/e5BXQJcr

#investing #cloud #AI

We’re excited to share that the UK’s former Chief Scientific Adviser for National Security, Prof. Alex van Someren FREng FIET, is joining Paladin Capital as a Strategic Advisor. He has served as a key figure in the UK’s technology, venture capital, and national security sectors for decades, and we are delighted to have him on the team. 

Alex joins Paladin Capital’s Strategic Advisory Group to lend his technical and market expertise to our investment thesis. Throughout his career, Alex has founded several tech companies with the goal of aiding democratic nations in securing their cyber and AI infrastructure. He has also served as a consultant to the €1 Billion NATO Innovation Fund and is a fellow of the Royal Academy of Engineering and the Institution of Engineering and Technology.

We’re proud to welcome Alex as we further our commitment to the European market and tackle the challenges posed by a rapidly evolving cyber and AI ecosystem. 

Find out more here: https://lnkd.in/eDy6BFBV

The Paladin Global Institute (PGI), led by President Kemba Walden and Senior Director Devin Lynch, is proud to share their new report: “The AI Tech Stack: A Primer for Tech and Cyber Policy.” Co-authored by Walden and Lynch, the paper lays out a digestible explanation of the intersecting domains of AI technology for tech practitioners.   

Walden and Lynch developed this paper to satisfy a need within the policy, cyber, and tech communities to better understand the different layers of AI and how we ensure this dynamic technology is grounded in trust, safety, and security. 

We hope you’ll take a few minutes to read this important report and share it with your colleagues working on tech and cyber issues: https://lnkd.in/g8hFnJBb

Today more than ever, we need an empowered leader to strengthen our national cybersecurity strategy and enhance private and public sector collaboration to address cyber threats facing the U.S. and our allies.
  
This week, two of Paladin’s Strategic Advisory Group members, RADM (Ret.) Mark Montgomery and former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis, urged members of the U.S. Senate to confirm Sean Cairncross as the nation’s next National Cyber Director.  

Paladin joins our distinguished advisors in supporting this nomination. Cairncross has a strong track record of public service and has demonstrated his commitment to advancing our U.S. national security.   

“There has never been such an important moment to have top leadership in place to drive the defense of America’s critical infrastructure from nation states and criminal gangs who each day initiate cyber-attacks to steal, disrupt, and destroy. Sean Cairncross is the right leader for the job, and we urge his quick confirmation as the next National Cyber Director,” said Michael Steed, Founder and Managing Partner of the Paladin Capital Group. 

Read Steed's statement from February following the announcement of Cairncross' nomination: https://lnkd.in/e_hKP5kj

Today, Paladin portfolio company and leader in Arm device virtualization Corellium announced its agreement to be acquired by Cellebrite, a powerhouse in digital intelligence and investigative analytics. Corellium helps developer and cybersecurity teams build, test, and secure mobile and IoT apps, firmware, and hardware through the power of virtualization.  

Paladin has recognized the unique potential of Corellium to secure customers’ mobile devices, leading a $25 million Series A round in 2021. The acquisition will have an enterprise value of up to $200 million, resulting in excellent returns on capital for Paladin’s investors. 

Corellium Co-Founder and CEO Amanda Gorton shared: “Paladin’s support played a pivotal role in helping us scale our technology and our mission — and ultimately, in reaching this milestone with Cellebrite. We’re deeply grateful for their belief in us.” 

Read more: https://lnkd.in/dC49T2uy

We are delighted to announce that we have led a $30M Series A funding of MIND™, the upcoming leader in data loss prevention (DLP), to fuel the Seattle-based startup’s growth.

In 2024, over 1.7 billion individuals were affected by data breaches, costing an average $5m. 

MIND’s AI-native solution prevents such sensitive data loss through its proprietary endpoint agents.

“AI is transforming how enterprises access and leverage data – but also how they lose it,” observes Paladin’s Gibb Witham. “As large language models make sensitive data instantly available to employees through natural language prompts, DLP has re-emerged as a critical, hair-on-fire problem.”

👉 Since emerging from stealth only 7 months ago, MIND has achieved 500% customer growth, with a growing roster of Fortune 1000 companies.

👉 The startup was named a top 10 finalist in the recent RSAC Conference Innovation Sandbox contest.

Crosspoint Capital Partners, Okta Ventures and YL Ventures also joined the round. 

Congrats to Eran Barak, Co-Founder and CEO, and the whole MIND team!
